proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months: each one-unit increase in ecog_ps corresponds to a decrease in mean pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Linear regression of pfs_months on ecog_ps: coef=-1.18 months/unit (SE 0.0156), Pearson r=-0.3204, p<1e-300. Higher ECOG strongly tied to shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV disease.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test of pfs_months by stage_iv: stage IV mean 3.6165 mo (n=14969) vs non-stage-IV 5.1528 mo (n=35031), diff -1.5363 mo, t=-66.6189, p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) have shorter pfs_months than patients without brain metastases.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: brain-mets mean 3.7496 mo (n=5090) vs no-brain-mets 4.7998 mo (n=44910); diff -1.0502 mo, p<1e-180.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with liver metastases (liver_mets=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Patients with bone metastases (bone_mets=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Patients with pleural effusion (pleural_effusion=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: liver-mets 4.7084 mo vs no-liver-mets 4.6901 mo; diff 0.0183 mo, p=0.554723. NOT significant.
Welch t-test: bone-mets 4.7235 mo vs no-bone-mets 4.6852 mo; diff 0.0383 mo, p=0.170438. Not significant.
Welch t-test: pleural-effusion 4.6642 mo vs none 4.698 mo; diff -0.0338 mo, p=0.278442. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ER-positive disease (er_positive=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than ER-negative disease.
PR-positive disease (pr_positive=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than PR-negative disease.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: PR+ 4.8235 mo vs PR- 4.4616 mo; diff 0.362 mo, p<1e-50.

proposed_hypotheses.text: HER2-positive disease (her2_positive=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than HER2-negative disease (main effect).
Trastuzumab is more beneficial in HER2-positive than HER2-negative patients (positive treatment_trastuzumab × her2_positive interaction on pfs_months).

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: HER2+ 4.3171 mo vs HER2- 4.776 mo; diff -0.459 mo, p<1e-60.

OLS pfs_months ~ treatment_trastuzumab * her2_positive — interaction coef -0.028 mo, p=0.694589; subgroup tx vs ctl effect in HER2+: -0.0792 mo (p=0.152968). NO benefit detected in HER2+ subgroup; trastuzumab × HER2+ interaction NOT significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: HER2-low disease (her2_low=1) is associated with different pfs_months than HER2-zero disease.
Sacituzumab govitecan provides greater PFS benefit in HER2-low patients than HER2-not-low (positive treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan × her2_low interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: HER2-low 4.7649 mo vs HER2-not-low 4.6504 mo; diff 0.1144 mo, p=1e-06. Statistically significant but tiny in magnitude.
OLS pfs_months ~ tx*her2_low — interaction coef -0.0376 mo, p=0.626152; in HER2-low subgroup tx effect -0.0307 mo (p=0.639357). Sacituzumab does NOT show enhanced benefit in HER2-low.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with any BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutation (brca_any=1) differ in pfs_months from BRCA-wild-type patients (main effect).
Olaparib provides greater PFS benefit in BRCA-mutated patients than BRCA-wild-type (positive treatment_olaparib × brca_any interaction on pfs_months).

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: BRCA-any 4.6199 mo (n=2499) vs BRCA-wt 4.6967 mo; diff -0.0768 mo, p=0.132119. Not significant.

OLS pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib * brca_any — interaction coef 0.4022 mo, p=0.020843 (significant). In BRCA-mutated subgroup, olaparib vs no-olaparib gives 0.3462 mo benefit (p=0.041423); in BRCA-wt subgroup effect is -0.0559 mo (p=0.142714).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tamoxifen has a stronger PFS benefit in ER-positive patients than ER-negative patients (positive treatment_tamoxifen × er_positive interaction).
Tamoxifen provides greater PFS benefit in postmenopausal patients than premenopausal patients (positive treatment_tamoxifen × postmenopausal interaction).

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ tam * er_positive — interaction coef -0.0582 mo, p=0.270095; in ER+ effect 0.0232 mo (p=0.449849); in ER- effect 0.0814 mo (p=0.030006). NO ER+ enrichment of tamoxifen benefit; interaction NOT significant.
Postmenopausal × tamoxifen interaction coef -0.0426 mo, p=0.392051. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Palbociclib has a stronger PFS benefit in ER-positive than ER-negative patients (positive treatment_palbociclib × er_positive interaction).
Palbociclib's benefit is greatest in ER-positive/HER2-negative patients (positive treatment_palbociclib × er_pos_her2_neg interaction).

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ palb * er_positive — interaction coef 1.5944 mo, p<1e-300. In ER+ subgroup palbociclib vs not = +1.577 mo (p<1e-300). In ER- subgroup effect -0.0175 mo (p=0.627192). HIGHLY significant ER+ enrichment.
OLS pfs_months ~ palb * er_pos_her2_neg — interaction coef 1.9297 mo, p<1e-300. In ER+/HER2- palbociclib vs not = +1.9238 mo. The single largest treatment-biomarker effect in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab provides greater PFS benefit in MSI-high patients than MSI-stable patients (positive treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction).
Pembrolizumab benefit differs by tp53 mutation status (treatment_pembrolizumab × tp53_mutation interaction).

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ pembro * msi_high — interaction coef 0.2171 mo, p=0.465626. In MSI-high subgroup pembro effect 0.1405 mo (p=0.637456, n_tx=86); in MSI-stable effect is -0.0766 mo. MSI-high subgroup small (n=498); no significant interaction.
Pembro × TP53 interaction coef -0.0488 mo, p=0.435673. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient on albumin_g_dl).

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ albumin_g_dl: coef 0.4988 mo per g/dL (SE 0.0221), Pearson r=0.1002, p<1e-100. Higher albumin → longer PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r(ldh_u_l, pfs_months)=-0.0164, p=0.000245. Higher LDH → shorter PFS (small effect, significant).
Pearson r(crp_mg_l, pfs_months)=-0.0033, p=0.460649. Not significant.
Pearson r(nlr, pfs_months)=0.0028, p=0.537232. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation between age_years and pfs_months).
Higher BMI (bmi) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r(age_years, pfs_months)=0.7015, p<1e-300. UNEXPECTED DIRECTION — older patients have LONGER pfs_months in this cohort (positive correlation, very strong: r≈0.70). Hypothesis refuted; direction reversed.
Pearson r(bmi, pfs_months)=0.0072, p=0.105034. Not significant.
Pearson r(weight_loss_pct_6mo, pfs_months)=-0.1155, p<1e-130. More weight loss → shorter PFS, as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Larger primary tumor size (tumor_size_cm) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher Ki67 proliferation index (ki67_pct) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r(tumor_size_cm, pfs_months)=0.0004, p=0.930797. Null.
Pearson r(ki67_pct, pfs_months)=-0.0914, p<1e-90. Higher Ki67 → shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue grade (fatigue_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher pain (pain_nrs) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher dyspnea grade (dyspnea_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher appetite-loss grade (appetite_loss_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher hemoglobin (hemoglobin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher platelets (platelets_k_ul) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher absolute neutrophil count (anc_k_ul) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher absolute lymphocyte count (alc_k_ul) is associated with longer pfs_months.
PIK3CA mutation (pik3ca_mutation=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than PIK3CA-wild-type.
TP53 mutation (tp53_mutation=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than TP53-wild-type.
HER2 amplification (her2_amplification=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-amplified.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: PIK3CA-mut 4.3281 mo (n=17365) vs WT 4.887 mo; diff -0.5589 mo, p<1e-140.
HER2-amp 4.7514 vs non-amp 4.6911; diff 0.0603 mo, p=0.382085. Null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: More prior lines of therapy (prior_lines_of_therapy) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Prior chemotherapy (prior_chemotherapy=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Prior immunotherapy (prior_immunotherapy=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Longer time since diagnosis (years_since_diagnosis) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Mean pfs_months differs by race_ethnicity category.
Mean pfs_months differs by insurance_type category.
Rural residence (rural_residence=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
More smoking (smoking_pack_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: OLS F-test pfs_months ~ C(race_ethnicity): F=0.8489, p=0.493944. Means: {'asian': 4.7355, 'black': 4.7166, 'hispanic': 4.7072, 'other': 4.7617, 'white': 4.6792}. Not significant.
OLS F-test pfs_months ~ C(insurance_type): F=0.3104, p=0.817894. Means: {'medicaid': 4.7064, 'medicare': 4.6921, 'private': 4.685, 'uninsured': 4.7329}. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Chronic kidney disease (chronic_kidney_disease=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Heart failure (heart_failure=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Interstitial lung disease history (interstitial_lung_disease_history=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Autoimmune disease (autoimmune_disease=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
At least one of the 25 SNP markers (snp_rs*) is associated with pfs_months at a Bonferroni-corrected significance threshold (alpha = 0.05/25 ≈ 0.002).

analyses.code: for c in snp_cols: stats.pearsonr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson screen across all 25 SNPs. Smallest p was snp_rs1801131 (r=-0.0099, p=0.02644); Bonferroni threshold 0.002083. NO SNP survives correction; hypothesis NOT supported — no SNP is significantly associated with PFS at corrected α.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment for ECOG, stage, mets, labs, biomarkers, and treatments, ecog_ps remains negatively associated with pfs_months.
After mutual adjustment, treatment_palbociclib has a positive coefficient on pfs_months (palbociclib remains an independent predictor of longer PFS).
After mutual adjustment, age_years remains positively associated with pfs_months (older = longer PFS persists adjusting for biology).

After mutual adjustment, the targeted-therapy main-effect terms for trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab, pembrolizumab, and tamoxifen are NOT significant predictors of pfs_months in the average patient.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS (n=50000, R²=0.78). ecog_ps coef -1.1709 mo (p=0.0, sig=True).
treatment_palbociclib adjusted coef 1.1032 mo (p=0.0). Independently positive.
age_years adjusted coef 0.1754 mo per year (p=0.0). Strongly positive even adjusting for biology.

Adjusted main-effect coefficients (mo): trastuzumab -0.0085 (p=0.516219), olaparib -0.0339 (p=0.053236), sacituzumab 0.0304 (p=0.08212), pembrolizumab -0.0229 (p=0.11752), tamoxifen -0.0119 (p=0.298381). All non-significant in main-effect form, supporting the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In HER2-NEGATIVE patients (negative control), trastuzumab does not improve pfs_months (null effect expected).
In BRCA-wild-type patients (negative control), olaparib does not improve pfs_months.
In ER-positive patients, palbociclib improves pfs_months substantially (positive control).
In MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab improves pfs_months (positive control); statistical power is limited (small subgroup).

analyses.result_summary: In her2_neg subgroup, trastuzumab vs no = -0.0512 mo (p=0.104291). Confirmed null.
In brca_wt subgroup, olaparib vs no = -0.0559 mo (p=0.142714). Confirmed null/slightly negative trend.
In ER+ subgroup, palbociclib vs no = +1.577 mo (p<1e-300). Strongly positive (positive control passes).
In MSI-high subgroup, pembro vs no = +0.1405 mo (p=0.637456, n_tx=86, n_ctl=412). Direction is positive (consistent with hypothesis) but power-limited; not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ECOG, stage, brain mets, liver mets, albumin, LDH, NLR, and age within the HER2-positive subgroup, trastuzumab still does not significantly improve pfs_months.
After adjusting for prognostic covariates within the BRCA-mutated subgroup, olaparib does not significantly improve pfs_months (i.e., the unadjusted +0.35-mo benefit attenuates with adjustment).
After adjustment within the ER+/HER2- subgroup, palbociclib provides a sizeable independent positive effect on pfs_months (>1 month).
After adjustment within the ER+ subgroup, tamoxifen does not significantly change pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: In HER2+ subgroup OLS pfs_months ~ trastuzumab + ECOG + stage + brain_mets + liver_mets + albumin + LDH + NLR + age (n=9060): trastuzumab coef 0.0124 mo (p=0.187143). Adjusted effect remains non-significant.
In BRCA-mut subgroup adjusted OLS (n=2499): olaparib coef 0.066 mo (p=0.453816). Adjustment knocks out the unadjusted +0.35-mo benefit; small subgroup limits power.
In ER+/HER2- subgroup adjusted OLS (n=28589): palbociclib coef +1.9228 mo (p<1e-300). Strong, robust positive effect — the standout finding of the analysis.
In ER+ subgroup adjusted OLS (n=34886): tamoxifen coef 0.0029 mo (p=0.878409). Tamoxifen still null after adjustment.
